blocks.body: Tenses help you say when something happens. Start with Present Simple, Present Continuous, and Past Simple.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"Tenses help you show when something happens: regularly, now, or in the past.","rules":[{"label":"Time meaning","color":"emerald","text":"Choose a tense to show the time or kind of action.","example":"every day, now, yesterday, last week"},{"label":"Present Simple","color":"blue","text":"Start with the tense for habits, facts, and repeated actions.","example":"I work every day. / She lives in Warsaw."},{"label":"Present Continuous","color":"amber","text":"Then move to actions happening now or around the present time.","example":"I am studying now. / They are working this week."},{"label":"Past Simple","color":"rose","text":"Then add the tense for finished actions in the past.","example":"I visited my aunt yesterday. / We watched a film last night."}]}

{"title":"1. Starter subsections","intro":"Begin with three core tenses for A1–A2.","items":[{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Present Simple","subtitle":"Forms, negatives, questions, and adverbs of frequency."},{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Present Continuous","subtitle":"Forms, negatives, questions, and time expressions."},{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Past Simple","subtitle":"Forms, negatives, questions, and time expressions."}]}

{"title":"2. In short","items":["Tenses show when an action or state happens.","Present Simple gives you the base for habits, facts, and repeated actions.","Present Continuous adds actions happening now or around the present time.","Past Simple is used for finished actions and events in the past.","Together, these three subsections cover the first starter block for A1–A2."]}
